definitions.definition: Used to describe habits, routines, and general truths, often with a sense of regularity or routine. It is formed using the base form of the verb (for I, you, we, they) or the base form with 's' or 'es' (for he/she/it).
Used to describe actions that are happening at the moment of speaking or actions that are temporarily true. It is formed using the base form of the verb + 'ing'.
Used to describe actions that started in the past and continue up to the present moment. It is formed using 'has/have' + the past participle of the verb.
definitions.example: I have studied English for three years.

definitions.definition: Used to describe actions that started in the past and continue up to the present moment, often with a sense of duration or a connection to the present. It is formed using 'has/have' + 'been' + the -ing form of the verb.
definitions.example: I have been studying English for three years.

definitions.definition: Used to describe completed actions in the past. It is formed using the base form of the verb (for I, you, we, they) or the base form with 'd' or 'ed' (for he/she/it).
Used to describe actions that were happening at a specific time in the past. It is formed using the past form of the verb + 'ing'.
definitions.example: I was studying English at 8 pm yesterday.

definitions.definition: Used to describe actions that happened before another action in the past. It is formed using 'had' + the past participle of the verb.
definitions.example: I had studied English before I moved to the US.

definitions.definition: Used to describe actions that started before another action in the past and continued up to that point. It is formed using 'had' + 'been' + the -ing form of the verb.
definitions.example: I had been studying English for two years before I moved to the US.

definitions.definition: Used to describe actions that will happen in the future. It is formed using 'will' + the base form of the verb.
Used to describe actions that will be happening at a specific time in the future. It is formed using 'will be' + the -ing form of the verb.
definitions.example: I will be studying English at 8 pm tomorrow.

definitions.definition: Used to describe actions that will be completed before another action in the future. It is formed using 'will have' + the past participle of the verb.
definitions.example: I will have studied English by the time I graduate.

definitions.definition: Used to describe actions that will start before another action in the future and continue up to that point. It is formed using 'will have been' + the -ing form of the verb.
definitions.example: I will have been studying English for two years by the time I graduate.

definitions.definition: The subject of the sentence performs the action described by the verb.
definitions.example: The teacher is teaching the students.

definitions.definition: The subject of the sentence receives the action described by the verb.
definitions.example: The students are being taught by the teacher.

definitions.definition: A verb that connects the subject to additional information, such as a predicate nominative or an object complement.
A verb that expresses physical or mental action.
A verb that takes an object, often expressing action or transfer.
A verb that does not take an object, often expressing a state or condition.
